Mention any two orally acting direct factor Xa inhibitors. 
Give one example each of following hypolipidaemic drugs acting by : (a) PPAR- α agonism [0.5] (b) Bile acid sequestration [0.5] 
Enumerate any two uses of d-penicillamine. Mention two disadvantages of selective cox-2 inhibitor. 
Give two examples of peripherally acting skeletal muscle relaxants having short duration of action. 
A pharmacokinetic study of a new drug is being conducted in healthy human volunteers. The half-life of the drug after administration by continuous IV Infusion is 10 hours. Which of the following best approximates the time for the drug to reach steady state? a) 20 hours b) 40 hours c) 60 hours d) 100 hours. 
Highly plasma protein bound drugs: a) Makes the drug short acting b) Have larger volume of distribution c) In case of overdose, they can be removed by haemodialysis d) Is not available for metabolism until free drug is metabolized or eliminated. 
Which of the following α blockers is selective α₂ blocker: a) Prazosin b) Dihydroergotamine c) Phenoxybenzamine d) Yohimbine.
